Isi Berita Clean: Perkembangan teknologi dan industri yang pesat telah memicu perubahan iklim yangsemakin ekstrem dalam beberapa dekade terakhir, sehingga isu ini menjadi salah satu topik utama dalam diskusi tentang kelangsungan hidup manusia di bumi. clean_text: over the past decade palm oil prices have fluctuated significantly due to changing demand adverse weather conditions and geopolitical tensions demand shifts especially from the food cosmetics and biofuel sectors have led to sharp price movement s while the covid-19 pandemic has further disrupted supply chains and labour availability however major palm oil producer bumitama agri sgx p8z has demonstrated resilience in addition to strong financial performance the company has provided attractive returns to its shareholders aligning with its mission statement it has also been recognised as one of the top dividend payers on the singapore exchange sgx s68 due to its solid fundamentals and appealing dividend yields for its fy2022, bumitama recorded an all time high dividend distribution of 7.8 cents based on a 55% payout ratio which translated to a 14% dividend yield for its latest fy2023, the company s dividend distribution stood at 6.8 cents with the payout ratio remaining robust at a 10% dividend yield this is on the back of sound cash flow and a healthy balance sheet executive director christina lim says that the company s decision to distribute special dividends is based on its surplus cash and low leverage ratio following the strong cash flow from operations in the past couple of years this is because bumitama remains consistent and meticulous in both its operational and financial management on top of the elevated palm oil price our key objective is to maximise shareholder value in the long term how to grow the business sustainably by optimising financial performance in the mid to long term paving the way for increasing dividends and capital gains we believe that shareholder value creation would best reward investors in the long term stellar performance see also propnex soars capturing greater market share amid challenging real estate climate recognised for its performance and strength bumitama has clinched three awards at this year s billion dollar club bdc for the food and beverages and food and drug retailing sector the planter not only clinched the best growth in profit after tax over three years weighted return on equity over three years it is also the overall sector winner lim highlights that the company has delivered a stellar financial performance over the past five years doubling its revenue since 2019 aside from tripling its earnings within the same time frame this she says is the result of a teamwork effort as it requires synergy among its 30,000 workers as well as another 30,000 indigenous farmers under the government mandated plasma scheme to achieve the uptrend at bumitama managing a plantation of our size is a daily challenge but it is made possible by upholding the culture and values instilled by our founder one such motto includes the time tested excellence through discipline which emphasises discipline and unity it is applied from top management to every staff member and field worker and reinforced daily to ensure everyone shares the same foundational principles says lim see also shaping the future of sustainable mobility the company is also hands on in its management approach constantly seeking ways to improve through research and development guided by its motto work together grow together and prosper together bumitama also focuses on optimising efficiency controlling costs and prudent management our 96-year old founder often reminds us that as we prosper so should our community operating out of mostly west and central kalimantan we ensure our presence benefits everyone through initiatives in education infrastructure and corporate social responsibility we also strive to ensure that the crude palm oil cpo that we produce continues to become affordable cooking oil for the community lim adds accelerated financial performance in 2h2024 can bumitama sustain its strong dividend distribution the company experienced a subdued first half due to muted production volumes from successive years of extreme weather and climate change along with ongoing cost pressures from fertiliser following a previous commodity spike despite the 4% setback in 2qfy2024, which ended june revenue quarterly profitability began to spike versus 1qfy2024 figures bumitama s gross profit net profit and ebitda for 2qfy2024 were up by 19% 61% and 41% q o q respectively attributable to rising prices and declining cost pressure core profit also climbed 43% q o q in 2qfy2024. lim expects bumitama to report accelerated financial performance in the second half of 2024 following favourable industry prospects for one the global oils and fats complex has tripled in consumption over the past three decades palm oil significantly outperformed the market with its market share rising from 16% to 32% over the same period while production volume has surged sixfold since 2019, however the growth rate of palm oil supply has not only slowed but a supply deficit has also been seen forming according to two global experts previously production grew at approximately 6% per year particularly during the growth period from 2010 to 2019. currently the growth has decelerated to about 1% per year additionally both oil world and the us department of agriculture usda are projecting a supply deficit among major edible oils in 2024, continuing into 2025. this anticipated deficit marks the first consecutive annual decline in palm oil stockpiles since the 1980s according to usda demand remains strong especially in indonesia which has been expanding consumption significantly historically considered a discount oil palm oil has recently traded at a premium over soybean and sunflower seed oils underscoring the current shortage and reinforcing bumitama s confidence in its market outlook against this backdrop bumitama is optimistic that the elevated price environment for palm oil will persist at least into next year this sustained price strength coupled with ongoing demand is expected to drive our accelerated financial performance in the upcoming periods says lim a key factor contributing to the projected supply deficit in palm oil is indonesia s increasing domestic consumption driven by its biodiesel mandate since the establishment of its palm oil fund in 2015, indonesia has consistently raised its biodiesel blending requirements despite initial scepticism due to past inconsistencies in execution between 2015 and 2024, the mandate has resulted in an increase of approximately 10 million to 11 million tonnes of palm oil demand for biodiesel in 2023, blending volume reached 12.25 million kiloliters 18% higher y o y this year indonesia is targeting a blending volume of 13.41 million kiloliters implying 9% y o y growth lim adds that the upcoming presidential transition from joko widodo to prabowo subianto is expected to bolster this trend further the next premier has announced plans to raise the biodiesel mandate to b40 40% biodiesel blend as early as january 2025, adding an extra 5% to the blending requirement this policy is anticipated to consume an additional two million tonnes of palm oil for biodiesel production this surge in domestic consumption is significant as indonesia s export volumes have declined from their peak levels despite being the world s largest producer of palm oil consequently the export share of indonesia s palm oil production has dropped from around 70% to below 60% currently the increased domestic use particularly for biodiesel acts as a catalyst for maintaining elevated price levels in the global market adds lim bumitama s asp stood at around idr12,000 kg for 1hfy2024. looking ahead the company expects this trend to continue lim notes that palm kernel pk prices have surged even more sharply than palm oil prices during this period in 2qfy2024, pk contributed approximately 9% to bumitama s total revenue the price ratio of pk to cpo stayed below 50% over the past eight quarters however it surpassed 55% in 2qfy2024 and is currently around 70% driven by a tight supply of lauric oils like coconut oil we anticipate pk s contribution to revenue will continue rising in the second half of the year she adds cost pressures particularly from fertilisers are also expected to decrease in the second half of the year due to a lag between market price changes and bumitama s cost accounting the company is now beginning to see costs subside the company expects these reduced costs to remain stable in the upcoming quarters which along with strong selling prices positions it towards accelerated financial performance bumitama berdaya aside from its financials bumitama is also mindful of sustainability working closely with organisations like the roundtable on sustainable palm oil rspo to ensure responsible practices late last year the company even achieved recognition for bumitama biodiversity and community project receiving the coveted rspo conservation leadership award this achievement is a testament to our long standing commitment to environmental stewardship in collaboration with international experts and local stakeholders including the regional government who acknowledged this biodiversity rich land as a protected conservation zone says lim the company has made a significant advancement in carbon footprint reduction working with its partners to connect the methane capture facility in its largest mill in riau to produce 3mws of green electricity for the local electricity grid this helps to reduce its carbon emissions intensity by 22.7% below its 2016 baseline bumitama has also made strides in its social endeavours meticulously designed and executed to foster the self reliance of the communities with whom it interacts within the past year the company deployed a wide ranging selection of initiatives under the bumitama berdaya provide support and effort campaign touching upon the aspects of education economic empowerment ecology health and humanitarian needs for example in partnership with akademi komunitas perkebunan yogyakarta bumitama initiated a mentorship programme to facilitate students access to the indonesian palm oil plantation fund management agency scholarship for aspiring talents in the palm oil industry by 2023, a total of 438 students had benefited from the programme our focus extends beyond merely maximising profits we embrace bumitama s triple bottomline approach while we continuously strive to enhance our operations we also prioritise sustaining our accomplishments through these local empowerment initiatives it really embodies the work together grow together and prosper together motto that we held so dear says lim

Dengan menggunakan pupuk ini, penyerapan hara oleh tanaman kelapa sawit dapat dioptimalkan, sehingga juga dapat mengurangi emisi gas rumah kaca. Teks_English: PT Astra Agro Sustainable Tbk Aali is committed to strengthening Astra Agro Sustainable's partnership with the palm oil growing community, ensuring sustainable supply chains and fair plantation practices. PT Astra Agro Lestari Tbk Aali emphasizes its commitment to strengthening partnerships between the company and the palm oil growing community. This step was taken to maintain the sustainability of the company's supply chain and ensure the implementation of sustainable plantation practices. Solid cooperation between the company and partner farmers is the main key. Astra Agro Chief Operations Officer Arief Catur Irawan explained that this partnership is very important. Strong collaboration will encourage the potential of strategic palm oil commodities to provide great benefits for many parties. The flow of fresh FFB fruit bunches from partners to companies will be smoother thanks to the cooperation that has been established to support this partnership. Astra Agro Sustainable continues to develop various initiatives, one of which is through the 2026 Partnership Gathering which took place in Yogyakarta on 12-15 January 2026. This event is a forum for strengthening relationships with partners from various regions in Indonesia. Sustainable continues to innovate to support effective and efficient partnerships. The company continues to develop the Siska partnership information system which makes it easier for partners to manage FFB delivery schedules and this application service allows partners to work more structured and productive. Apart from Siska, the company also expands the application of mill graders, a technology-based quality control mechanism. This system allows the results of FFB quality assessments to be more objective and consistent. The application of this technology is proof of the company's seriousness in building a fair partnership. Arief Catur Irawan emphasized that for partners the presence of this technology is a strong indicator. This shows the company's commitment to building a partnership based on justice and fairness. It is hoped that this openness of innovation can increase trust and transparency. One of Astra Agro Sustainable's concrete efforts in strengthening partnerships is through the 2026 Partnership Gathering activity. This event was held in Yogyakarta from 12 to 15 January 2026. This meeting carried the theme of strengthening partnerships for sustainable business. This activity was attended by partners from various regions in Indonesia. Participants came from Aceh, Riau, Jambi, Central Kalimantan, South Kalimantan, East Kalimantan, West Sulawesi to Central Sulawesi The presence of partner representatives from various regions shows the broad scope of the partnership. Astra Agro Chief Commercial Officer Veronica Lusi Herdiyanti emphasized the importance of maintaining strong partnerships. This must be done with integrity and mutual commitment, including in the work safety aspect. Equal collaboration is the main foundation. Veronica Lusi Herdiyanti emphasized that the sustainability of the palm oil industry can only be achieved through equal collaboration. This collaboration must be established between companies and grower partners. Strong partnerships are the key to the future of the industry by prioritizing transparency, technological innovation and ongoing assistance from Astra Agro Sustainable. optimistic that this partnership will continue to grow and become stronger in the future. These principles are the main pillars in every interaction. This commitment reflects Astra Agro Sustainable's vision of not only operating as a business, the company also strives to have a positive impact on the environment and surrounding communities. Harmonious partnership will support the long-term sustainability goals of Asian Agri and Apical. Affirm a strong commitment to sustainable palm oil management in line with UN sustainable development for the economic contribution and welfare of farmers. PT Astra Agro Lestabil Tbk Aali is committed to increasing palm oil productivity through rejuvenation of plants and superior seeds without expanding land. See Their innovative strategy is that PT Astra Agro Lestari Tbk is fully committed to traceability of the palm oil supply chain down to the farmers through digital innovation. Curious about how they will make it happen, Menkop is optimistic that cooperation with the RSPO will strengthen the corporatization of palm oil farmers while strengthening palm oil production from upstream to downstream. East Kalimantan palm oil prices will fall in the early January 2026 period due to weakening CPO. Check out the details of FFB prices and strategies so that farmers don't suffer losses and maintain price stability.

Teks_English: CNBC Indonesia An incident occurred on Sunday 24 12 2023 in the industrial area managed by PT Indonesia Morowali Industrial Park IMIP in Central Sulawesi. One of the furnaces of the stainless steel smelter processing and refining facility operated by PT Indonesia Tsingshan Stainless Steel PT ITS experienced a severe explosion at around 06.15 WITA on Sunday 24 12 2023. The furnace that exploded was furnace no.41. This incident also claimed the lives of 13 workers, consisting of 9 Indonesian workers and 4 workers from China who died. Meanwhile, 46 victims were injured, generally due to being exposed to hot steam. The management of PT Indonesia Morowali Industrial Park IMIP has formed a team to handle the impact of work accidents at the factory location. The management of PT IMIP has covered all costs for post-accident care and treatment of the victims as well as compensation for the families of the victims. We have also handed over one victim's body to the victim's family, said media relations head PT IMIP Dedy. Kurniawan in a written statement on Sunday 24 12 2023. Responding to this incident, the Coordinating Ministry for Maritime Affairs and Investment, the Coordinating Ministry for Maritime Affairs and Fisheries, immediately ordered regional parties to prioritize handling victims. The Coordinating Ministry for Maritime Affairs and Investment has asked regional parties to prioritize handling victims and their families, said the Deputy for Coordinating Investment and Mining, Coordinating Ministry for Maritime Affairs and Fisheries, Septian Hario Seto in a written statement on Sunday 24 12 2023. Seto said that regional parties had formed a special team responsible for post-accident handling. including emotional support for the families of the victims and a thorough analysis of the causes of the accident, he said, this step is to ensure that all aspects of this accident are handled seriously and professionally. We ask for a thorough investigation to find out the causes of this accident and that the regional authorities are committed to transparency and will share information as soon as it is available, he said. Indonesia Tshingshan Stainless Steel PT ITS is mostly owned by Chinese investors, 50% of PT ITS shares are owned by Tsingshan Holding Group Company Limited, then 20% are owned by Ruipu Technology Group Company Limited, 10% Tsingtuo Group Co Ltd, 10% Hanwa Company Limited and the remaining 10% are owned by investors from Indonesia, namely PT Indonesia Morowali Industrial Park IMIP, the Ministry of Energy and Mineral Resources said PT ITS is the holder of an IUI industrial business permit. So the supervisory authority is under the Ministry of Industry based on data from the Ministry of Industry in 2018, in the IMIP industrial area, it produces up to 3 million tons of stainless steel per year, of which 1 million tons per year is produced by PT ITS, the rest is produced by PT Sulawesi Mining Investment SMII 1 million tons and PT Indonesia Guang Ching Nickel and Stainless Steel Industry GCNs 1 million tons in the Morowali Tsingshan industrial area and Bintang Empat Group is said to have invested around US 6 billion. From this investment in Morowali, Indonesia has become one of the stainless steel producers that is taken into account in the global market

Teks_English: Indonesian mining issuer PT Adaro Minerals Indonesia Tbk ADMR denies greenwashing of the aluminum smelter project which uses a coal power plant in North Kalimantan, as is known, Adaro Energy is seeking funding from international banks to finance an aluminum project worth USD 2 billion. This has been protested by environmental activists. Greenwashing is a marketing strategy to image an environmentally friendly company but is considered to be deceiving customers. The company campaigns for its products to be environmentally friendly or simply following trends. President Director of Adaro Minerals Indonesia Christian Ariano Rachmat explained that the company will transition to green energy regarding the reason that the raw material is still from coal because the hydro plant will only be ready in 2030. We want to say that we are not greenwashing. We clearly said why this aluminum plant will be built from coal, why from coal because hydro will only be ready in 2030, he said, quoted Thursday 11 5. Christian revealed that 75% of aluminum smelters in the world still use coal plants, the rest is 25% use hydro or water-based power plants in Russia, Canada and Brazil. One day we want to make what is called green aluminum but it takes time to get there until our hydro is so he said. According to him, the Adaro Minerals aluminum smelter being built in Indonesia aims to no longer import aluminum ingots, because currently Indonesia is still importing almost 1 million tons of aluminum. The construction of the project is hoped to help with import dependence. We don't lie, people say that this is green aluminum, it will be green aluminum, but there are steps, he said, he stressed production capacity. Domestic aluminum will be able to save imported foreign exchange which will reach USD 2.5 billion per year. If the smelter is completed then the need for aluminum of up to 1 million tons will be met. We don't want to be green, we want to be green, we definitely want to be green, but everything has a process, he concluded.

Teks_English: Indonesian banking continues to demonstrate its commitment to implementing environmental social and governance ESG principles. This can be seen in the total disbursement of green credit which continues to increase. Data compiled by the OJK financial services authority from bank reports from state-owned banks shows that total green credit or financing throughout 2024 reached IDR 1,452 trillion. Chief Executive of Banking Supervision, Dian Ediana Rae, said that this achievement cannot be separated from the OJK's encouragement through various guidelines such as climate risk management scenario analysis, CRMS and the Indonesian Sustainable Finance Taxonomy. TKBI version 2. However, Dian realizes that the prospect of green credit still faces a number of challenges, such as the high risk profile, the long repayment period and the limited number of sustainable projects in Indonesia, plus projects like this, according to Dian, there is still a lack of data and information transparency. In addition, the world's commitment to the zero emissions target is also starting to loosen, as can be seen in the departure of a number of United States banks from the Paris Agreement and the Net Zero Banking Alliance. In the midst of the slackening commitment of a number of companies, PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Persero TBK is still posting increases. distribution of green credit in the first quarter of 2025. It is recorded that the bank for the little people has distributed green credit amounting to IDR 89.9 trillion, an increase of 8.18% on an annual basis YoY. Director of Human Capital and Compliance Bri A Solichin Lutfiyanto detailed the sectors financed including sustainable natural resource management worth IDR 61.16 trillion, environmentally friendly products IDR 7.80 trillion, renewable energy IDR 6.47 trillion and green transportation worth IDR 3.55 trillion. believes that active contribution to achieving SDGS sustainable development goals will have a positive impact not only on the environment but also in strengthening the resilience of the national economy, said Solichin. Overall, Solichin continued, BRI's sustainable financing portfolio in that period reached IDR 796 trillion, which includes green financing for MSME financing as well as investment in ESG-based corporate bonds. This amount, said Solichin, is equivalent to 64.16% of BRI's total financing and investment portfolio of corporate bonds in the first quarter of 2025. This value takes up a portion of almost 25% of the total credit portfolio. Cimb Niaga on a bank only basis was recorded at Rp. 160 trillion. CIMB Niaga Business Banking Director Rusly Johannes said that the highest distribution was channeled to the micro, small and medium MSME sector amounting to Rp. 24.35 trillion. This value is equivalent to 43% of the total distribution of green credit. CIMB Niaga views that sustainable financing through KKUB is a very prospective sector in line with the increasing awareness of business actors regarding the importance of environmental and social aspects, said Rusly. Apart from that, Rusly continued. This financing is deliberately aligned with the commitment to implement a zero emission target by 2060 and the integration of ESG principles in all business activities of Cimb Niaga Bank. Rusly also said that he had initiated the one house one tree movement or planting one tree for one house of Cimb Niaga home ownership credit debtors to ensure the right direction in the development of sustainable financing. Rusly said Cimb Niaga had implemented various internal policies such as sustainable finance policy procedures, making a list of sectors with high environmental risk and actively monitoring the financing portfolio according to the KKUB categories regulated by the authorities. financial services in OJK Regulation No. 51 of 2017. These steps are a concrete manifestation of CIMB Niaga's support for the transition to a low-carbon Paris Agreement economy and achieving SDGs sustainable development goals through a more sustainable financial ecosystem, concluded Rusly

The smelter industry is basically an industrial sector with a high level of complexity in production processes and technology, so knowledge and skills and qualifications are required from workers in carrying out the operations of a smelter. This technological complexity also causes the smelter industry to have a high level of risk and is closely related to K3 occupational health and safety. Therefore, K3 training and certification for workers in the smelter industry is very important in maintaining safety and reducing the number of work accidents. With this training and certification, workers will be trained to understand and identify potential dangers and safety procedures that must be implemented when operating a smelter. K3 certification not only provides benefits for workers but also for companies managing smelters in carrying out their operations by ensuring that workers have adequate knowledge and skills in terms of company safety. It can reduce the risk of losses both financial and non-financial. Apart from that, good implementation of K3 standards will help companies comply with government regulations. getting stricter regarding work safety to continue to improve standards and create a culture of K3 within the company environment PT Gunbuster Nickel Industry GNI regularly holds K3 certification programs, especially the SIO heavy equipment operator license certification until the end of 2024, the company continues to carry out this agenda, including SIO dump trucks, SIO excavators, SIO loaders, SIO boilers, SIO furnaces, SIO mobile cranes, turbine technician certification, turbine technician certification, electrical technician certification, steam aircraft and pressure vessel technician certification, high building labor certification, class D fire extinguisher certification, K3 certification for lifting aircraft and transport various gni K3 certification programs are part of the company's commitment to ensure safe and efficient operations and meet increasingly stringent industry standards. K3 is a top priority in the mineral and coal industry, especially in the nickel ore smelter processing industry sector. K3 certification not only provides in-depth technical knowledge for technicians but also ensures they are able to apply appropriate safety procedures in everyday work situations. These various K3 certification activities are in collaboration with PJK3 occupational health and safety services companies that have been certified by the Indonesian Ministry of Manpower, Indonesian Ministry of Manpower, Head of PT GNI Corporate Communication Mellysa Tanoyo emphasized that work safety culture must be part of the life of every worker, therefore implementing K3 is the main principle so that all business processes can run well. Commitment to prioritizing work safety and increasing awareness of K3 in every business process. We will continue to carry out work safety as our main priority. Mellysa said some time ago. Through this K3 certification program, PT GNI wants to ensure that every technician or worker has sufficient ability and knowledge to carry out their duties safely and efficiently. She emphasized that work safety is a shared responsibility and needs to always be improved. the article is that K3 is an investment in the company's future. Teks_English: Sustainable financing or credit from banks plays an important role in accelerating the realization of a green economy in Indonesia. Sustainable credit distribution can also strengthen the implementation of environmental social and governance aspects of ESG for banks referring to the roadmap for sustainable finance in Indonesia stage i2015-2019 from the OJK financial services authority. Sustainable finance is all activities in the financial services industry resulting from harmony between social economic and environmental interests. The activities included in it carry several principles such as low carbon emissions, promoting environmentally friendly investment and supporting the concept of sustainable development. The implementation of sustainable finance in the regulated financial services sector industry. In OJK Regulation No. 51 POJK. 03 2017 concerning the implementation of sustainable finance for issuer financial service institutions and public companies, since the issuance of this regulation, interest in financial service institutions including banks in implementing sustainable finance has increased. Green financing policies. One of the banks that consistently applies sustainable banking principles is PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk. Bri in environmentally sound financing. Bri implements green financing or green financing. This bank coded as BRI shares distributes financing by emphasizing the fulfillment of environmental sustainability aspects by business players. Bri has a loan portfolio guideline. Including aspects of environmental considerations in distributing credit, including a negative list. This guide also contains sectoral financing policies for the palm oil and pulp paper sectors in these two sectors. BRI not only requires debtors to pay attention to environmental aspects but also social aspects to avoid or minimize overlap with communities around sites and conservation areas. BRI requires medium and small corporate debtors to have Indonesian Sustainable Palm Oil certification. The application of sustainable banking principles that BRI has implemented is expected to support sustainable business growth and contribute to realizing the company's vision of becoming the most valuable banking group in Southeast Asia champion. of financial inclusion in 2025, said the main director of bri sunarso to kontan co id empowering SMEs, the realization of bri's ESG-based sustainable financing is increasing every year until the end of September 2023. bri succeeded in disbursing credit amounting to rp1,250.72 trillion or grew 12.53% compared to the same period the previous year year on year yoy of the entire bri credit portfolio, esg credit occupied a portion of 66.1% of the achievement of esg credit at the end The third quarter of 2023 recorded growth of 11.89% yoy to IDR 750.91 trillion. This figure strengthens BRI as the bank with the largest sustainable credit portfolio in Indonesia. BRI distributes ESG-based credit to two main sectors, namely environmentally friendly business activities, kubl and SMEs. A number of KUBLs that received BRI green funding include renewable energy projects, prevention and control of pollution, environmentally friendly transportation, green building, biodiversity conservation and sustainable waste management. As a bank that focuses on serving the MSME sector, the growth of BRI ESG credit, especially supported by the empowerment of MSMEs, BRI has various empowerment platforms and credit schemes that are tailored to the development of micro business actors in order to support the improvement of their business to a feasible and bankable level. Empowerment programs are tailored to the journey or literacy journey of the MSMEs in question, starting from basic literacy to digital literacy. We know that what MSME actors really need is educational assistance, which means really accompanying how MSMEs run their businesses. We are making this journey even now at the ultra micro level, increasing to micro, increasing to small and medium and so on, said the Director of Small and Medium Business. Bri Amam Sukriyanto, several of Bri's superior UMKM empowerment programs include the Brilliant Village My Cluster, My Life Rumah BUMN and UMKM Export BrilliantPreneur. Until October 2023, there are 2,843 Brilliant Villages and 18,685 business clusters spread throughout Indonesia, more than 11,000 trainings by 54 Rumah BUNs and no less than 700 MSMEs participating in UMKM Export BrilliantPreneur. Each superior program has its own uniqueness. Each Brilliant Village, for example, is a village empowerment program to create role models in business development with the spirit of optimizing resources. It is hoped that villages that become role models can be replicated in other places. This year, BRI is targeting an additional 1,000 new villages. All of these strategies are then supported by 7,980 branches, 666,038 Brilink agents and more than 1,000 Smile outlets and Brimo super apps.

Teks_English: Schneider Electric completed the 2021-2025 Schneider Sustainability Impact SSI program with a score of 8 86 out of 10. This five-year program is the company's foundation in encouraging sustainability performance in aspects of social climate and governance as well as supporting the energy transition of customers and business partners. President Director of Schneider Electric Indonesia Timor Leste Martin Setiawan said that the implementation of SSI also has a direct impact on business strategy in the country. Completion of the 2021 Schneider Electric Sustainability Impact program. 2025 is a journey of transformation that we can truly feel in Indonesia, he said in an official statement on Thursday 5 3 2026. For us, sustainability is not just a global commitment but a business strategy that encourages performance to increase the competitiveness of national industry while providing a positive impact on Indonesian society. Globally, until the end of 2025, Schneider Electric has helped customers save and avoid 862 million tons of CO emissions, exceeding the initial target of 800 million tons. This achievement was driven by the use of more efficient energy management and automation solutions in the building industry sector. and infrastructure from the supply chain side, the company is running a zero carbon project involving 1,000 strategic suppliers. This program has contributed to a 56% reduction in suppliers' co-operational emissions. Apart from that, 98% of strategic suppliers have met decent work standards including protecting workers' rights and ethical labor practices in social aspects. Schneider Electric is expanding its Access to Energy program which has reached more than 61 million people globally since 2009. The company has also developed an energy management training program which has been attended by more than 1 million people to support workforce readiness in the energy transition, sustainability strategy and government relations. Schneider Electric Director Donald Situmorang emphasized that the next phase will focus on strengthening contributions to national targets, the foundation that we have built starting from partnerships with stakeholders to decarbonization initiatives and talent development as the main capital for moving towards 2030. In the future, the company will continue to utilize technology and innovation to support the national energy transition agenda, expand cross-sector collaboration and ensure that energy and digital transformation can provide inclusive and sustainable benefits for all. Schneider Electric's sustainability approach has also received recognition from a number of global ESG rating agencies, including Platinum Ecovadis CDP Climate. change a list as well as the top ranking in the social benchmark and gender benchmark according to the world benchmarking alliance. In the future, the company is preparing a follow-up phase after SSI 2021-2025 with a focus on strengthening the decarbonization of the energy digitalization industry as well as cross-sector collaboration to support Indonesia's energy transition target towards 2030.

Menurut Ferdinand Hutahaean, pengamat energi dari Energy Watch Indonesia, program co-firing yang dilakukan di pembangkit listrik tenaga uap (PLTU) menjadi contoh nyata bahwa peralihan dari energi fosil ke energi terbarukan dapat dilakukan secara bertahap dan terukur, serta memberikan dampak langsung yang positif bagi masyarakat. Dengan menggunakan teknologi co-firing yang memungkinkan pembakaran dua jenis bahan bakar, yaitu batubara dan biomassa, diharapkan dapat mengurangi ketergantungan pada batu bara dan mengarah pada penggunaan energi yang lebih berkelanjutan.

Teks_English: The co-firing program at the PLTU steam power plant is increasingly showing concrete results not only in supporting the energy transition but also in creating new community-based economic value. Energy observer from Energy Watch Indonesia Ferdinand Hutahaean considers this program to be proof that the transition from fossil energy to renewable energy can be carried out in measurable stages and has a direct impact on the people. According to him, co-firing, namely burning two types of fuel, coal and biomass, is a rational step to reduce dependence on coal without disrupting the reliability of electricity supply. The co-firing program not only strengthens the new renewable energy mix but also revives the economy at the village level. Biomass from plantation agricultural waste and community forest products has created a new supply chain that absorbs local workers and opens up business opportunities for cooperatives and MSMEs. PLN data shows that by the end of September 2025, biomass supply for co-firing needs has reached 1.7 million tons, approaching the cumulative target of 2.2 million tons. This supply is projected to exceed the annual target of 3 million tons in line with the increase in cooperation contracts with local suppliers, 000 tons which are still in the procurement process if all contracts are realized, the total supply is estimated to reach 5.5 million tons or 185 percent of this year's target is a strong signal that the national biomass supply system is starting to run effectively. In addition to the co-firing village bioenergy program, the biodes also shows great business potential in the green energy sector. This project has a net present value NPV of IDR 557.4 million, an internal rate of return IRR of 45.54% and a benefit cost ratio of BCR 1.03, with a payback period of only around 3.2 years, with an efficient financial structure, the biodes program is not only economically viable but also attractive for private investors, said Ferdinand. This program is estimated to be able to provide income of up to IDR 10 billion per year for villages while creating a new source of sustainable income. The government is also expanding the development of biomass-based electricity projects for the purpose of exporting energy. This diversification is considered important to strengthen national competitiveness amidst the global trend towards clean energy. The government's steps are in line with the global goal of achieving net zero emissions by 2060, while ensuring that the energy transition brings real benefits to communities at the grassroots, he concluded.

Teks_English: Apply ESG Bri records the largest sustainable financing in Indonesia worth IDR 796 trillion. PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Persero Tbk or BRI continues to demonstrate its commitment to supporting sustainable development through the implementation of ESG environmental social and governance principles in all business lines. This step not only creates added value for all stakeholders but also strengthens BRI's position as the bank with the largest sustainable financing portfolio in Indonesia. This commitment is reflected in BRI's sustainable financing portfolio, which has reached IDR 796 trillion by the end of the quarter. i-2025. This amount is equivalent to 64.16 percent of the total BRI financing portfolio. This portfolio consists of social loans amounting to Rp. 700.6 trillion, most of which are distributed to micro, small and medium SMEs throughout Indonesia. Green financing, green loans of Rp. 89.9 trillion for the environmentally friendly business activity sector, Kubl, as well as investment in ESG-based corporate bonds, Rp. 5.5 trillion. The main director of BRI, Hery Gunardi, explained that ESG principles have been implemented significantly in every aspect of BRI's business, from policy formulation to Implementation in all operational lines of each policy, he said, was prepared by considering the balance between business potential and its impact on social and environmental aspects. This approach is part of BRI's long-term strategic direction in building a tough and sustainable business, said Hery in his press statement on Tuesday 15 7 2025. Through the distribution of sustainable financing, he continued, BRI not only creates economic value and provides financial impact to customers but also contributes to achieving sustainable development goals. BRI's support for SDGs is realized through the integration of sustainability principles in its financing strategy and business model. The company's focus is directed at expanding access to inclusive finance in the transition to clean energy as well as strengthening the economic capacity of the community through financing that encourages productivity and job creation. Increasing the ESG rating. BRI's real commitment to sustainable practices is reflected in the significant increase in the ESG ranking at the international level based on the sustainability assessment. BRI succeeded in reducing the ESG risk score from 20.9 in the medium risk category in 2021 to 17.8 in the low risk category in 2024. Meanwhile, MSCI has consistently given BRI an A rating since 2022 to 2024. In the 2024 global ESG S P assessment, BRI recorded an increase in the ESG score to 75 with a position in the 95th percentile, and was named a member of the 2024 Sustainability Yearbook member, a recognition of superior sustainable financial practices in the global banking sector. In addition to international recognition, BRI is also recognized nationally as one of the public companies with the highest score in the ASEAN Corporate Governance Scorecard of 110.2 and achieved the title of Most Trusted Company from CGPI. 2024 with a score of 95.31. BRI has also been a permanent constituent in the FTSE4Good ASEAN 5, Sri Kehati and IDX ESG Leaders index since March 2021. These various recognitions reflect BRI's strategic role in driving inclusive, sustainable and socially responsible business transformation.

Teks_English: PT Bank Mandiri Tbk BMRI said that the distribution of sustainable financing or sustainable financing for the company had reached Rp. 264 trillion from total credit of Rp. 1,113.9 trillion in the first quarter of 2024. This financing consisted of green financing amounting to Rp. 130 trillion and social financing amounting to Rp. 134 trillion until March 2024. Specifically, social financing was distributed to MSMEs, Senior Vice President ESG Group Head of Bank Mandiri, Citra Amelya Pane, said that green financing had contributed. almost 25% of total credit and is in accordance with the provisions of the OJK financial services authority. He views the opportunities for green financing in Indonesia, including the circular economy, which is still wide open, so Bank Mandiri fully supports the government's efforts to encourage green financing and hopes that this portion of financing will continue to grow. This was conveyed by the image in the Green Investments Powering the Circular Economy in Indonesia held by Green Investments Powering the Circular Economy in Indonesia in Jakarta, Thursday 4 7. In supporting the achievement of the net zero emission framework, Bank Mandiri also implements sustainability which consists of three pillars, including sustainable finance. operations and sustainability beyond banking so that this state-owned bank does not only focus on aspects of sustainable financing as part of the business but as a whole within the Mandiri group, both in social activities and social impact through the CSR corporate social and environmental responsibility program at Bank Mandiri. The principle is how to embed it into the way of work, daily work must change and the daily business must also change the image. Therefore, Bank Mandiri is committed to encouraging green financing or environmentally friendly green financing with a green financing contribution reaching 30% by March 2024. For Bank Mandiri itself, we already have a current commitment. Green financing in the Indonesian market is 30% financed by Bank Mandiri as of March 2024, said Citra. In addition, Bank Mandiri has followed the direction of the national development planning agency Bappenas in supporting the circular economy with a contribution of around 15%. Bank Mandiri is also committed to continuing to increase the percentage contributing to the circular economy because the company sees big opportunities in Indonesia and wants to support the country in terms of renewable energy. Indonesia has a lot to learn from countries in Europe and the UK, for example for the development of geothermal solar panels and electric vehicles in the energy transition. Teks_English: North Konawe kponline secretary of the DPW FSPMI Southeast Sulawesi Province Alauddin enthusiastically socialized FSPMI to one of the workers of a mining company in North Konawe. On Wednesday, the socialization activity was carried out directly during the busy agenda ahead of May Day 2026. Alauddin explained the history of the vision and role of FSPMI as a trade union organization that consistently fights for workers' rights and welfare, especially in the industrial and mining sectors. In addition to introducing FSPMI, he invited these workers to join the federation of metal and mining workers' unions. The Indonesian Metal Workers Union or SPLP FSPMI, let's join so that the struggle of mining sector workers will be better protected considering that the work risks are quite high, said Alauddin. According to Alauddin, the mining sector in Southeast Sulawesi has big challenges ranging from work safety, decent wages to the status of work relations, which still mostly use contract systems and outsourcing. Therefore, the existence of labor unions is important as a tool of collective struggle. We cannot fight alone. By joining a union, our voices are more heard by companies and are more serious when dealing with organizations, not individuals. Alauddin stressed that SPLP FSPMI already has educational advocacy tools and a national network. to international members, members will receive legal assistance, training and access to the latest employment information. This socialization is part of the FSPMI base expansion program in the North Konawe nickel industrial area. DPW FSPMI Sultra is targeting the formation of new PUKs in several mining and smelter companies throughout 2026. The workers we met responded positively to the invitation and expressed their interest in further discussions with other colleagues, Yanto

Teks_English: follow social media and get various benefits home news bri URL successfully copied sustainable financing portfolio reaches IDR 787 9 trillion click to read sustainable financing portfolio reaches IDR 787 9 trillion 14 May 202 4 14 09 Jakarta PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Persero Tbk or BRI recorded that the sustainable financing portfolio until the end of the first quarter of 2024 reached IDR 787.9 trillion or the equivalent of 66.2 percent of the total credit disbursed and Bri government bond investment portfolio Bri A Compliance Director Solichin Lutfiyanto said the company continues to be committed to implementing sustainable finance through the implementation of environmental social and governance ESG, distributing sustainable credit, Bri remains focused on distributing credit to the MSME sector which reached IDR 700.1 trillion, Solichin said, as reported on Tuesday, May 14 2024. Apart from MSME, sustainable credit also includes financing to the green sector amounting to IDR 83.1 trillion. This green sector consists of the biological natural resources management sector and environmentally friendly land use worth IDR 54.84 trillion, green transportation IDR 11.78 trillion, renewable energy IDR 6.29 trillion and other environmentally friendly business activity sectors amounting to IDR 10.17 trillion. In addition, the company notes that BRI also has an ESG-based government bond investment portfolio worth IDR 4.7 trillion. From the funding side, BRI posted total bonds with an outstanding of IDR 37.2 trillion, including BRI's green bonds worth IDR 13.5 trillion which were launched in stages in 2022, 2023 and 2024. Apart from that, BRI also received funding through sustainability linked loans and inclusivity based securities. The company said that raising funds and distributing credit to the green sector is one of BRI's efforts to achieve NZE's net zero emissions target in 2050. BRI's greenhouse gas emissions calculations also carry out various initiatives starting from calculating greenhouse gas emissions for scope 1, scope 2, scope 3 which refers to international scope 3 emission standards. What BRI calculates includes financed emissions, namely indirect emissions originating from companies that receive financing from BRI. From the operational side, BRI is carrying out an operational eco efficiency program which aims to reduce company emissions originating from operational activities. This is done through the use of 119 eco friendly cars and 150 electric motorbikes as office operational vehicles as well as the installation of solar panels in 93 work units as a low emission energy alternative. Not only that, BRI also seeks to expand resilience efforts to climate change by involving customers and the community through the BRI initiative to plant programs involving brilliant village customers and communities. This area has succeeded in planting 904,196 trees and reaching 2,593 villages throughout Indonesia with the potential to absorb carbon dioxide reaching 780,606 kg CO2e

Teks_English: BRI's green financing reached IDR 89.9 trillion in the midst of the green banking transformation of PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Persero Tbk or BRI showing its commitment to supporting sustainable development through increasing green financing. Green financing until the end of the first quarter of 2025, BRI's sustainable financing portfolio was recorded as continuing to grow and had reached IDR 89.9 trillion. This figure increased by 8.18 percent year on year yoy in line with the increasingly strong focus of the national banking industry on green transformation. If detailed, BRI's green financing portfolio includes various strategic sectors such as sustainable natural resource management amounting to IDR 61.16 trillion, environmentally friendly products IDR 7.80 trillion and renewable energy IDR 6.47 trillion. Apart from that, there is financing in the green transportation sector amounting to IDR 3.55 trillion for green buildings and other environmental projects. BRI's green financing is also in line with OJK financial services authority regulation number 18 of 2023 concerning the issuance and requirements for debt securities and sukuk based on overall sustainability. BRI is the bank with the largest sustainable financing portfolio in Indonesia by nominal value. reaching IDR 796 trillion by the end of quarter I-2025. This figure comes from green financing, financing for micro, small and medium enterprises, MSMEs as well as investment in corporate bonds based on environmental social and governance, ESG. This amount is equivalent to 64.16 percent of the total portfolio of financing and investment in corporate bonds. Bri Director of Human Capital Compliance, Bri A Solichin Lutfiyanto explained that the distribution of green financing shows Bri's seriousness in being an agent of change towards a green economy. Sustainable finance is important to bridge the gap in financial support for sustainability initiatives, he said in a press release, Thursday 12 6 2025. Bri continued. Solichin plays an important role in providing financing for debtors who are transitioning to low-carbon and sustainable practices while supporting the government's efforts to expand green financing solutions. ESG realization related to the implementation of the ESG agenda i-2025, with 39.66 percent of it coming from ESG-based instruments. The composition includes the issuance of green bonds in three stages worth IDR 13.5 trillion and a sustainability linked loan of 1 billion US dollars. The issuance of the sustainability linked loan is the first in the Asian financial sector to support increasing the composition of micro financing as part of an adaptive strategy in portfolio management. BRI also issues inclusivity-based debt securities such as medium term notes and subordinated bonds which are allocated to MSMEs, ultra-micro corporations and low-income individuals. By integrating ESG principles throughout the financing and financing value chain, BRI continues to strengthen its role as a bank with a comprehensive sustainability mission
